title: International Statistical Institute

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(**ISI**) is a professional association of statisticians. At a meeting of the Jubilee Meeting of the [Royal Statistical Society](/wiki/Royal_Statistical_Society "Royal Statistical Society"), statisticians met and formed the agreed statues of the International Statistical Institute.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was founded in 1885, although there had been international statistical congresses since 1853.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The institute has about 4,000 members from government, academia, and the private sector. The affiliated associations have membership open to any professional statistician.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Professional association of statisticians

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies excessively on [references](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") to [primary sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research#Primary,_secondary_and_tertiary_sources "Wikipedia:No original research")**. Please improve this article by adding [secondary or tertiary sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research#Primary,_secondary_and_tertiary_sources "Wikipedia:No original research").  *Find sources:* ["International Statistical Institute"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22International+Statistical+Institute%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

International Statistical Institute[[1]](#cite_note-UIA-1)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Formation | 1885 |
| Type | Statistical society |
| Headquarters | [The Hague](/wiki/The_Hague "The Hague"), [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") |
| President | [Xuming He](/wiki/Xuming_He "Xuming He") |
| Website | [www.isi-web.org](https://www.isi-web.org/) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q139020#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

The **International Statistical Institute** (**ISI**) is a professional association of statisticians. At a meeting of the Jubilee Meeting of the [Royal Statistical Society](/wiki/Royal_Statistical_Society "Royal Statistical Society"), statisticians met and formed the agreed statues of the International Statistical Institute.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was founded in 1885, although there had been international statistical congresses since 1853.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The institute has about 4,000 members from government, academia, and the private sector. The affiliated associations have membership open to any professional statistician.

The institute publishes a variety of books and journals, and holds an international conference every two years. The biennial convention was commonly known as the ISI Session; however, since 2011, it is now referred to as the ISI **World Statistics Congress**.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The permanent office of the institute is located in the [Statistics Netherlands (CBS)](https://www.cbs.nl/en-gb) building in the [Leidschenveen-Ypenburg](/wiki/Leidschenveen-Ypenburg "Leidschenveen-Ypenburg") district of [The Hague](/wiki/The_Hague "The Hague"), in the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"). It was established in 1913 to preserve documents and findings as well as publishing an international statistical yearbook periodically. The ISI does not disclose its membership fees until an applicant has created an account.

The ISI is built upon statutes that aim at establishing strong statistical relationships between countries through research, publications, and teachings by professional statisticians.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) The ISI contains seven associations that each have their own form of government, specified journals, and tasks. Each association works individually, but also closely together to further obtain the ISI's goals.

The institute has also collaborated with the [United Nations Statistical Commission](/wiki/United_Nations_Statistical_Commission "United Nations Statistical Commission") over the years on numerous topics, as they have shared interests in the statistical community. These collaborations and overlaps have occurred most commonly over statistical ethics to be used worldwide, as well as having members be a part of both organizations at some point in time. [[1]](https://unstats.un.org/unsd/statcom/50th-session/resources/documents/ISI%20Contribution-rev.pdf)

## Specialized associations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Statistical_Institute&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Specialized associations")]

ISI serves as an umbrella for seven specialized Associations:[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

* [Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability](/wiki/Bernoulli_Society_for_Mathematical_Statistics_and_Probability "Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability") (BS)
* [International Association for Statistical Computing](/wiki/International_Association_for_Statistical_Computing "International Association for Statistical Computing") (IASC)
* [International Association for Official Statistics](/wiki/International_Association_for_Official_Statistics "International Association for Official Statistics") (IAOS)
* [International Association of Survey Statisticians](/w/index.php?title=International_Association_of_Survey_Statisticians&action=edit&redlink=1 "International Association of Survey Statisticians (page does not exist)") (IASS)
* [International Association for Statistical Education](/wiki/International_Association_for_Statistical_Education "International Association for Statistical Education") (IASE)
* [International Society for Business and Industrial Statistics](/w/index.php?title=International_Society_for_Business_and_Industrial_Statistics&action=edit&redlink=1 "International Society for Business and Industrial Statistics (page does not exist)") (ISBIS)
* [The International Environmetrics Society](/w/index.php?title=The_International_Environmetrics_Society&action=edit&redlink=1 "The International Environmetrics Society (page does not exist)") (TIES)

## Committees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Statistical_Institute&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Committees")]

ISI Committees fall under one of three categories: operational, special interest, and outreach.

Current *Special Interest Groups* are:

* Agricultural sciences
* [Astrostatistics](/wiki/Astrostatistics "Astrostatistics")
* [History of statistics](/wiki/History_of_statistics "History of statistics")
* Professional ethics[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* Promotion of statistics in the life sciences
* Risk analysis
* Sports statistics
* Statistics of travel and tourism
* Women in statistics

## Journals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Statistical_Institute&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Journals")]

ISI publishes the following journals:

* *[Bulletin of the International Statistical Institute](/wiki/Bulletin_of_the_International_Statistical_Institute "Bulletin of the International Statistical Institute")*
* *International Statistical Review*
* *[Statistical Theory and Method Abstracts](/w/index.php?title=Statistical_Theory_and_Method_Abstracts&action=edit&redlink=1 "Statistical Theory and Method Abstracts (page does not exist)")*
* *[Bernoulli](/wiki/Bernoulli_(journal) "Bernoulli (journal)")*
* *[Computational Statistics & Data Analysis](/wiki/Computational_Statistics_%26_Data_Analysis "Computational Statistics & Data Analysis")*
* *[Statistics Education Research Journal](/w/index.php?title=Statistics_Education_Research_Journal&action=edit&redlink=1 "Statistics Education Research Journal (page does not exist)")*
* *[Statistics Surveys](/wiki/Statistics_Surveys "Statistics Surveys")*

## Karl Pearson Prize

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Statistical_Institute&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Karl Pearson Prize")]

The Karl Pearson Prize was commenced by the ISI in 2013 to acknowledge contributions, which must be a research article or book published within the last three decades, on statistical theory, methodology, practice, or applications. The prize was named after English statistician [Karl Pearson](/wiki/Karl_Pearson "Karl Pearson"). It is bestowed biennially at the ISI World Statistics Congress. The winner of the prize receives 5,000 euros and gives the Karl Pearson Lecture.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

[Peter McCullagh](/wiki/Peter_McCullagh "Peter McCullagh") and [John Nelder](/wiki/John_Nelder "John Nelder") were the winners of the inaugural Karl Pearson Prize "for their monograph Generalized Linear Models (1983)".[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Presidents of ISI

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Statistical_Institute&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Presidents of ISI")]

The organization has had thirty-eight presidents.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
The current president is [Xuming He](/wiki/Xuming_He "Xuming He").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

* 1885 – 1899: Sir [Rawson W. Rawson](/wiki/Rawson_W._Rawson "Rawson W. Rawson") ([![England](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/b/be/Flag_of_England.svg/40px-Flag_of_England.svg.png)](/wiki/England "England"))
* 1899 – 1908: [Karl von Inama-Sternegg](/w/index.php?title=Karl_von_Inama-Sternegg&action=edit&redlink=1 "Karl von Inama-Sternegg (page does not exist)") [[de](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Theodor_Inama_von_Sternegg "de:Theodor Inama von Sternegg")] ([![Austria-Hungary](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c9/Flag_of_Austria-Hungary_%281867%E2%80%931918%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Austria-Hungary_%281867%E2%80%931918%29.svg.png)](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary"))
* 1909 – 1920: [Luigi Bodio](/wiki/Luigi_Bodio "Luigi Bodio") ([![Kingdom of Italy](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Flag_of_Italy_%281861-1946%29_crowned.svg/40px-Flag_of_Italy_%281861-1946%29_crowned.svg.png)](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy"))
* 1923 – 1931: [Albert Delatour](/w/index.php?title=Albert_Delatour&action=edit&redlink=1 "Albert Delatour (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Delatour "fr:Albert Delatour")] ([![France](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c3/Flag_of_France.svg/40px-Flag_of_France.svg.png)](/wiki/France "France"))
* 1931 – 1936: [Friedrich Zahn](/w/index.php?title=Friedrich_Zahn&action=edit&redlink=1 "Friedrich Zahn (page does not exist)") [[de](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Friedrich_Zahn_(Statistiker) "de:Friedrich Zahn (Statistiker)")] ([![Weimar Republic](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Flag_of_Germany_%283-2%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Germany_%283-2%29.svg.png)](/wiki/Weimar_Republic "Weimar Republic")/![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/Flag_of_Germany_%281933%E2%80%931935%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Germany_%281933%E2%80%931935%29.svg.png)/[![Nazi Germany](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/Flag_of_Germany_%281935%E2%80%931945%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Germany_%281935%E2%80%931945%29.svg.png)](/wiki/Nazi_Germany "Nazi Germany"))
* 1936 – 1947: [Armand Julin](/w/index.php?title=Armand_Julin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Armand Julin (page does not exist)") ([![Belgium](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/92/Flag_of_Belgium_%28civil%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_Belgium_%28civil%29.svg.png)](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"))
* 1947 – 1947: [Walter Francis Willcox](/wiki/Walter_Francis_Willcox "Walter Francis Willcox") ([![United States](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Flag_of_the_United_States_%281912-1959%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States_%281912-1959%29.svg.png)](/wiki/United_States "United States"))
* 1947 – 1953: [Stuart A. Rice](/wiki/Stuart_A._Rice_(statistician) "Stuart A. Rice (statistician)") ([![United States](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Flag_of_the_United_States_%281912-1959%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States_%281912-1959%29.svg.png)](/wiki/United_States "United States"))
